reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All 3 gates passed: auctionCallback is live (BaseAuction.sol:449), entry point maps to UNTRUSTED bidders, AuctionBidder.sol is in scope. An attacker holding AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE can call bid() with a solution containing Call{target: ERC20, data: approve(attackerEOA, type(uint256).max)}, which executes unconditionally in auctionCallback._multiCall (no target or selector filtering), granting unlimited allowance on the AuctionBidder's token balances, enabling a full drain via transferFrom. Downgraded from Critical to High because exploitation requires AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE (admin-granted, not permissionless); the scope defines Critical as direct theft with no privileged access, while High covers significant loss via callback exploitation — the pattern explicitly listed in severity_classification examples.
